Networks violate one core assumptions conventional regression models: independence observations. analysing dyadic relationships, observations share actors therefore statistically dependent. Quadratic Assignment Procedure (QAP) addresses problem evaluating coefficient significance repeated node-label permutations. Instead relying conventional sampling assumptions, QAP compares observed coefficients reference distribution generated randomly permuted versions network. qapreg package provides formula-based interface fitting QAP regression models directly network objects.

code: # Development version devtools::install_github("YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/qapreg")

text: begin creating small directed network. package converts network dyadic data set internally.
code: library(qapreg) library(network) #>  #> 'network' 1.20.0 (2026-02-06), part of the Statnet Project #> * 'news(package="network")' for changes since last version #> * 'citation("network")' for citation information #> * 'https://statnet.org' for help, support, and other information  net <- network.initialize(5, directed = TRUE)  add.edges(   net,   tail = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 4),   head = c(2, 3, 4, 5, 5) ) head(qap_dyads(net)) #>   y i j #> 1 0 2 1 #> 2 0 3 1 #> 3 0 4 1 #> 4 0 5 1 #> 5 1 1 2 #> 6 0 3 2

text: simplest model uses sender receiver identifiers. output contains: Estimated coefficients Model-based standard errors QAP permutation standard errors One-sided two-sided QAP p-values
code: fit <- qaplogit(   net = net,   formula = y ~ i + j,   reps = 100,   seed = 123,   verbose = FALSE )  summary(fit) #>             estimate model_se qap_se qap_p_less qap_p_greater qap_p_two_sided #> (Intercept)  -1.4001   2.0556 0.0837     0.0396        0.9901          0.0396 #> i            -0.5114   0.4446 0.2599     0.0693        0.9505          0.0990 #> j             0.5114   0.4446 0.2599     1.0000        0.0198          0.0495 #>  #> QAP settings: #>   Repetitions: 100 #>   Directed: TRUE #>   Missing dyads: omit #>   Missing model values: omit #>   Failed repetitions: 0 #>  #> Permutation covariance matrix: #>             (Intercept)       i       j #> (Intercept)      0.0070  0.0032 -0.0032 #> i                0.0032  0.0675 -0.0675 #> j               -0.0032 -0.0675  0.0675

text: Standard S3 methods available.
Many network analyses include dyadic predictors : Geographic distance Similarity measures Shared group membership Prior interactions Dyadic covariates supplied matrices. can included directly model.
code: set.seed(123)  distance <- matrix(   runif(25),   nrow = 5,   ncol = 5 ) fit2 <- qaplogit(   net = net,   formula = y ~ i + j + distance,   dyadic_covariates = list(     distance = distance   ),   reps = 100,   seed = 123,   verbose = FALSE )  summary(fit2) #>             estimate model_se qap_se qap_p_less qap_p_greater qap_p_two_sided #> (Intercept)  -0.9733   2.2161 1.5662     0.6535        0.3663          0.6832 #> i            -0.5222   0.4526 0.3275     0.0891        0.9307          0.1089 #> j             0.5814   0.4829 0.3576     0.9604        0.0594          0.0792 #> distance     -1.0923   1.7915 3.0264     0.2574        0.7624          0.5842 #>  #> QAP settings: #>   Repetitions: 100 #>   Directed: TRUE #>   Missing dyads: omit #>   Missing model values: omit #>   Failed repetitions: 0 #>  #> Permutation covariance matrix: #>             (Intercept)       i       j distance #> (Intercept)      2.4529  0.0718  0.0657  -4.5103 #> i                0.0718  0.1073 -0.1120   0.0125 #> j                0.0657 -0.1120  0.1279  -0.3106 #> distance        -4.5103  0.0125 -0.3106   9.1591

text: package distinguishes two forms missingness.
Missing edges network controlled : Available options :
code: missing_dyads = "omit" "omit" "fail" "zero"

text: Missing values model variables handled using: Available options :
code: na_action = "omit" "omit" "fail" "pass"

text: package provides helper functions comparing multiple QAP models.
code: compare_netlogit(model1, model2) qap_netlogit_compare(model1, model2)

text: QAP coefficient interpreted way corresponding coefficient underlying regression model. key difference lies significance assessment. Rather assuming independent observations, significance evaluated permutation distribution generated repeatedly relabelling network nodes. Consequently, QAP p-values generally appropriate network data conventional model-based p-values.

Krackhardt, D. (1988). Predicting networks: Nonparametric multiple regression analysis dyadic data. Social Networks, 10(4), 359–381. Dekker, D., Krackhardt, D., & Snijders, T. . B. (2007). Sensitivity MRQAP tests collinearity autocorrelation conditions. Psychometrika, 72(4), 563–581.

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
text: qapreg R package fitting Quadratic Assignment Procedure (QAP) regression models network data. package provides modern formula interface QAP analysis network objects, allowing researchers estimate dyadic regression models accounting dependence structure inherent network data.

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
text: Formula-based model specification Logistic QAP regression via qaplogit() Support dyadic covariate matrices Permutation-based significance testing Standard S3 methods (print, summary, coef, vcov, confint) Integration network package

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
code: remotes::install_github("thomas-u-grund/qapreg")

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
text: Standard regression models assume observations independent. network data, dyads share actors therefore statistically dependent. violates conventional inference assumptions can lead misleading p-values. QAP addresses problem repeatedly permuting node labels comparing observed coefficients resulting reference distribution.

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
code: library(qapreg) library(network)  net <- network.initialize(5, directed = TRUE)  add.edges(   net,   tail = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 4),   head = c(2, 3, 4, 5, 5) )  fit <- qaplogit(   net = net,   formula = y ~ i + j,   reps = 100,   seed = 123,   verbose = FALSE )  summary(fit)

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
text: Dyadic predictors can supplied matrices.
code: distance <- matrix(   runif(25),   nrow = 5,   ncol = 5 )  fit <- qaplogit(   net = net,   formula = y ~ i + j + distance,   dyadic_covariates = list(     distance = distance   ),   reps = 100,   seed = 123,   verbose = FALSE )  summary(fit)

title: QAP Regression for Network Objects
code: coef(fit)  vcov(fit)  confint(fit)

what: Compare qapreg and netlogit estimates — compare_netlogit
title: Compare qapreg and netlogit estimates — compare_netlogit
text: Compares coefficients two-sided QAP p-values fitted qapreg object equivalent sna::netlogit() model.

title: Compare qapreg and netlogit estimates — compare_netlogit
code: compare_netlogit(   object,   net,   reps = NULL,   dyadic_covariates = NULL,   missing_dyads = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Compare qapreg and netlogit estimates — compare_netlogit
text: object object class "qapreg", usually returned qapreg() qaplogit(). net original network object used fit object. reps Optional number permutations sna::netlogit() comparison. Defaults object$reps. dyadic_covariates Optional named list dyadic covariate matrices. match covariates used fit object. missing_dyads Optional missing-dyad handling rule comparison. Defaults object$missing_dyads. seed Optional random seed used fitting comparison model.

title: Compare qapreg and netlogit estimates — compare_netlogit
text: data frame comparing terms, coefficients, two-sided QAP p-values. full sna::netlogit() object stored "netlogit" attribute.

title: Compare qapreg and netlogit estimates — compare_netlogit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- qaplogit(net, y ~ Grade_i + Grade_j, reps = 100) compare_netlogit(fit, net = net, reps = 100) } # }

"qap" uses permutation-based QAP standard errors. "model" uses model-based standard errors. ... Additional arguments, currently ignored.

what: Create dyadic data from a network object — qap_dyads
title: Create dyadic data from a network object — qap_dyads
text: Converts network object dyadic data frame suitable QAP regression. outcome variable named y. Vertex attributes added sender receiver variables using _i _j suffixes. Edge attributes user-supplied dyadic covariates added dyadic variables.

title: Create dyadic data from a network object — qap_dyads
code: qap_dyads(   net,   directed = NULL,   dyadic_covariates = NULL,   missing_dyads = c("omit", "fail", "zero") )

title: Create dyadic data from a network object — qap_dyads
text: net network object. directed Logical; whether treat network directed. NULL, directedness taken net using network::.directed(). dyadic_covariates Optional named list dyadic covariate matrices. matrix must dimensions n n, n number vertices net. missing_dyads Character string specifying missing dyads outcome network handled. "omit" drops missing outcome dyads, "fail" stops missing dyads present, "zero" treats missing dyads zero.

title: Create dyadic data from a network object — qap_dyads
text: data frame one row per dyad least columns y, , j. returned data frame also contains attributes including dyads, ymat, n, directed, missing_dyads, observed_mask.

title: Create dyadic data from a network object — qap_dyads
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(network)  net <- network::network(matrix(rbinom(25, 1, 0.2), 5, 5), directed = TRUE) dat <- qap_dyads(net) head(dat) } # }

what: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
title: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
text: Fits equivalent QAP logistic regression using sna::netlogit() comparison package's implementation.

title: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
code: qap_netlogit_compare(   net,   formula,   reps = 100,   dyadic_covariates = NULL,   missing_dyads = c("omit", "fail", "zero"),   seed = NULL )

title: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
text: net network object. formula model formula using internally generated outcome y. reps Number QAP permutations. dyadic_covariates Optional named list dyadic covariate matrices. missing_dyads Character string specifying missing outcome dyads handled. One "omit", "fail", "zero". seed Optional random seed used fitting comparison model.

title: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
text: fitted object returned sna::netlogit().

title: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
text: function intended primarily diagnostic validation aid. sna::netlogit() operates full adjacency matrices, exact equivalence easiest missing dyads missing_dyads = "zero" used.

title: Compare a QAP specification with sna::netlogit — qap_netlogit_compare
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(network) library(sna)  mat <- matrix(rbinom(100, 1, 0.2), 10, 10) diag(mat) <- 0 net <- network::network(mat, directed = TRUE)  qap_netlogit_compare(net, y ~ i + j, reps = 50) } # }

title: Logistic QAP Regression — qaplogit
text: Fits logistic QAP regression model. function supports package's formula interface netlogit-style matrix interface.

title: Logistic QAP Regression — qaplogit
code: qaplogit(   net,   formula,   reps = 100,   directed = NULL,   dyadic_covariates = NULL,   missing_dyads = c("omit", "fail", "zero"),   na_action = c("omit", "fail", "pass"),   seed = NULL,   save_permutations = TRUE,   save_permutation_ses = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   intercept = TRUE,   mode = c("digraph", "graph"),   diag = FALSE,   nullhyp = c("qap", "qapy", "classical"),   test.statistic = c("beta", "z-value"),   tol = 1e-07,   ... )

title: Logistic QAP Regression — qaplogit
text: net network object, response adjacency matrix using netlogit-style interface. formula model formula, matrix/array/list predictors using netlogit-style interface. reps Number QAP permutations. directed Logical; whether network directed. dyadic_covariates Optional named list dyadic covariate matrices. missing_dyads Handling missing dyads: "omit", "fail", "zero". na_action Handling missing model values: "omit", "fail", "pass". seed Optional random seed. save_permutations Logical; store permuted coefficients. save_permutation_ses Logical; store permuted standard errors. verbose Logical; print progress messages. intercept Logical; include intercept netlogit-style models. mode Character; "digraph" directed networks "graph" undirected networks. diag Logical; currently accepted compatibility. Diagonal dyads modelled qapreg. nullhyp Character; currently "qap" "qapy" use QAP-style response-network permutations. "classical" fits observed logistic regression without interpreting QAP p-values. test.statistic Character; accepted compatibility. Current QAP p-values based coefficients. tol Tolerance passed glm.control(). ... Additional arguments passed glm().

title: Logistic QAP Regression — qaplogit
text: object class "qapreg".

what: qapreg: QAP regression for statnet network objects — qapreg-package
title: qapreg: QAP regression for statnet network objects — qapreg-package
text: Tools preparing dyadic data network objects estimating regression models Quadratic Assignment Procedure (QAP) inference.

title: Quadratic Assignment Procedure regression — qapreg
text: Fits regression model dyadic network data evaluates coefficient significance using node-label permutations Quadratic Assignment Procedure (QAP).

title: Quadratic Assignment Procedure regression — qapreg
code: qapreg(   net,   formula,   reps = 100,   directed = NULL,   dyadic_covariates = NULL,   missing_dyads = c("omit", "fail", "zero"),   na_action = c("omit", "fail", "pass"),   fit_fun = stats::glm,   fit_args = list(family = stats::binomial()),   seed = NULL,   save_permutations = TRUE,   save_permutation_ses = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   parallel = FALSE,   ncores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1) )

title: Quadratic Assignment Procedure regression — qapreg
text: net network object. formula model formula. response usually y, internally generated dyadic outcome. Predictors may include vertex attributes _i _j suffixes, edge attributes, supplied dyadic covariates. reps Number QAP permutations. directed Logical; whether treat network directed. NULL, directedness taken net using network::.directed(). dyadic_covariates Optional named list dyadic covariate matrices. matrix must dimensions n n. missing_dyads Character string specifying missing outcome dyads handled. One "omit", "fail", "zero". na_action Character string specifying missing values model variables handled. One "omit", "fail", "pass". fit_fun Model-fitting function. Defaults stats::glm(). fit_args Named list additional arguments passed fit_fun. seed Optional random seed used generating permutations. save_permutations Logical; TRUE, store permuted coefficient estimates returned object. save_permutation_ses Logical; TRUE, store model-based standard errors permutation returned object. verbose Logical; TRUE, print progress messages permutations. parallel Logical; whether run permutations parallel. ncores Number CPU cores use parallel = TRUE.

title: Quadratic Assignment Procedure regression — qapreg
text: object class "qapreg", list containing observed coefficients, model-based standard errors, QAP standard errors, permutation covariance matrix, one-sided two-sided QAP p-values, observed fitted model, information QAP settings.

title: Quadratic Assignment Procedure regression — qapreg
text: QAP inference preserves network dependence repeatedly permuting node labels refitting model permuted outcome network. P-values computed empirical permutation distribution using plus-one correction. function deliberately flexible: changing fit_fun fit_args, users can fit models logistic regression, provided fitted model supports stats::coef() , ideally, stats::vcov().

title: Quadratic Assignment Procedure regression — qapreg
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(network)  mat <- matrix(rbinom(100, 1, 0.2), 10, 10) diag(mat) <- 0 net <- network::network(mat, directed = TRUE)  fit <- qapreg(net, y ~ i + j, reps = 50, verbose = FALSE) print(fit) } # }
